The Senate has passed a reconciliation bill using a 'current policy' baseline, which extends the 2017 TCJA individual tax cuts. President Trump signed the bill on July 4, 2025. The bill, known as the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA), modifies and extends several tax provisions.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025, makes domestic content requirements mandatory for obtaining the IRA's Production Tax Credits (45Y). The law introduces accelerated repeal schedules, shortens eligibility deadlines, tightens domestic content rules, and imposes new foreign entity of concern (FEOC) restrictions. Specifically, the OBBBA harmonizes the domestic content requirements under Section 48E with those under Section 45Y for projects beginning construction on or after June 16, 2025.

The domestic content requirements for obtaining the IRA's Investment Tax Credits (48Ε) have been made mandatory. President Trump signed H.R.1, the 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act,' on July 4, 2025, which amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits and imposes specific domestic content percentages for the clean electricity investment credit (section 48E).

The House of Representatives passed a reconciliation bill (H.R. 1) on May 22, 2025, which amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits, including the Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (Section 45X). The bill introduces Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) restrictions that would disallow credits for taxpayers identified as specified foreign entities or foreign-influenced entities, and for projects receiving material assistance from prohibited foreign entities after December 31, 2025. This effectively requires the use of domestic materials or subcomponents for the Advanced Manufacturing Production Tax Credits (45X) starting in 2025.
